lede: A look at March 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Mitchell Watt and Elton John, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Belarus
events.desc: Protesters demanding a new election in Belarus, following the rigged 2006 Belarusian presidential election, clash with riot police. Opposition leader Aleksander Kozulin is among several protesters arrested.

events.title: European Union
events.desc: The European Union's Veterinarian Committee bans the export of British beef and its by-products as a result of mad cow disease (Bovine spongiform encephalopathy).

events.title: Faisal of Saudi Arabia
events.desc: Faisal of Saudi Arabia is shot and killed by his nephew.

events.title: Triangle Shirtwaist Factory fire
events.desc: In New York City, the Triangle Shirtwaist Factory fire kills 146 garment workers.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: American Patriots conduct a Raid on Tybee Island, primarily seeking to capture runaway slaves who sought refuge with British forces stationed there.

events.title: Chorale cantata (Bach)
events.desc: Bach's chorale cantata "Wie schön leuchtet der Morgenstern, BWV 1", is first performed on the Feast of the Annunciation, coinciding with Palm Sunday.

events.title: Saturn
events.desc: Saturn's largest moon, Titan, is discovered by Christiaan Huygens.

events.title: Yongle Emperor
events.desc: The Yongle Emperor of Ming China launches the first of his military campaigns against the Mongols, resulting in the fall of the Mongol khan Bunyashiri.

events.title: Pope Constantine
events.desc: Election of Pope Constantine following the death of Pope Sisinnius. He would be the last pope to visit Constantinople until 1967.

events.title: Venice
events.desc: Italian city Venice is founded with the dedication of the first church, that of San Giacomo di Rialto on the islet of Rialto.

births.name: Elton John
births.line: English singer-songwriter, pianist, producer, and actor

births.name: Naftali Bennett
births.line: Israeli politician, 13th Prime Minister of Israel

deaths.text: Alexander Kluge, German author, film director and public intellectual
Faisal of Saudi Arabia, Saudi Arabian king
Frédéric Mistral, French lexicographer and poet, 1904 Nobel Prize laureate
Íñigo López de Mendoza, 1st Marquis of Santillana, Spanish poet and politician

observances.text: Anniversary of the Arengo and the Feast of the Militants (San Marino)
Christian feast days: Feast of the Annunciation
Christian Saints' days Ælfwold II of Sherborne
Christian Saints' days Blessed Marie-Alphonsine Danil Ghattas
Christian Saints' days Omelyan Kovch (Ukrainian Greek Catholic Church)
